footer.tagline:
Artificial Intelligence Network Systems Laboratory.
Innovating at the edge of possibility.

hero.subCopy: INSLAB builds adaptive AI systems, AIoT architectures, and MLOps pipelines — bridging research and real-world deployment across intelligent networks.

mission.title: Advancing the intersection of AI & Network Systems
mission.body: INSLAB is dedicated to creating scalable, secure, and intelligent solutions for next-generation connectivity. We don't just research. We build the architectural foundations for 6G and beyond.

researchAreas.title: Core Research Areas
researchAreas.subtitle: Pillars of scientific investigation at INSLAB.

areas.title: Intelligent Networking
areas.description: Optimizing network performance using advanced machine learning algorithms. We focus on 6G protocols, dynamic resource allocation, and self-healing network architectures.

areas.title: Distributed AI
areas.description: Developing decentralized AI frameworks for edge computing environments. Our work includes federated learning optimization and privacy-preserving computation.

areas.title: Cyber-Physical Systems
areas.description: Securing critical infrastructure through robust AI-driven defense mechanisms. We analyze IoT vulnerabilities and develop real-time intrusion detection systems.

items.title: INSLAB Wins Best Paper Award at ICC 2024
items.description: Our team's research on "Federated Learning Optimization for Edge Networks" was recognized for its contribution to efficient edge AI processing.

items.title: Spring 2024 Workshop on AI Security
items.description: INSLAB hosted a workshop bringing together industry experts from Google and Microsoft to discuss robust AI model security practices.

hero.title: Selected Publications
hero.body: Our research spans artificial intelligence, network systems, and cybersecurity. Explore representative papers and the themes shaping INSLAB's recent academic output.

hero.title: Join INSLAB
hero.body: We are always looking for passionate and curious minds to join our team. Explore opportunities for graduate and undergraduate research positions.

points.title: Cutting-Edge Research
points.description: Work on state-of-the-art problems in AI, 6G networking, federated learning, and IoT security with real-world impact.

points.title: Collaborative Environment
points.description: Join a supportive lab culture with regular seminars, code reviews, and mentoring from experienced researchers.

points.title: Publication Support
points.description: Receive guidance to publish at top-tier venues like IEEE ICC, TNSM, and Access with comprehensive support throughout the process.

requirements: B.S. in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, or related field
Interest in AI, networking, or IoT research
English proficiency for reading and writing papers
tracks.timeline: Applications accepted for Spring and Fall semesters

requirements: Currently enrolled as a junior or senior
Basic understanding of machine learning or networking
Commitment of at least one semester
Willingness to participate in lab seminars

items.question: What research topics can I work on?
items.answer: INSLAB focuses on three main areas: Intelligent Networking (6G, SDN/NFV), Distributed AI (federated learning, edge AI), and Cyber-Physical Systems (IoT security, anomaly detection). You can explore topics within these areas based on your interests.

items.question: Is funding available for graduate students?
items.answer: Yes, graduate students are supported through research project funding, university scholarships, and teaching assistantships. Details are discussed during the admission process.

items.question: Do I need prior research experience?
items.answer: Prior experience is helpful but not required. We value motivation, curiosity, and willingness to learn. Undergraduate research experience or relevant project work is a plus.

items.question: What is the typical weekly schedule?
items.answer: Graduate students participate in weekly lab seminars, individual research meetings with the advisor, and collaborative coding sessions. The lab encourages a balanced and productive research rhythm.

apply.title: How to Apply
apply.description: To express your interest in joining INSLAB, please send an email to Prof. Daeyoung Kim with the following documents attached.
documents: Brief statement of research interests (1 page)
Any relevant publications or project portfolios (optional)
